title: eryngium campestre var. virens
intro: Eryngium campestre var. virens, also known as the prickly sea holly, is a perennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Eryngium campestre var. virens include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be made by infusing its leaves and roots in water or a solvent like ethanol, and have been used to treat a range of health conditions.
